extraction.one_line_summary: BUITRAGO Santiago attacked alone from breakaway on final climb 8km from finish after crashing at 80km to go, held off KÄMNA by 25 seconds
tactical_patterns: Van der Poel attacked with 65km remaining on first Cat-1 climb, gaming breakaway dynamics before final climb selection began
Bahrain Victorious paced GC group while their own rider Buitrago was clear uproad, tactical luxury allowing dual outcomes
Kämna bridged to van der Poel then dropped him on final climb, but went too early at 8km downhill finish
Landa attacked over summit but Carapaz/Hindley matched easily, suggesting podium gap now unbridgeable despite bonus second opportunities gone

rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: positive: recovered from 80km crash to win shows exceptional durability, previously second-placed indicates consistent climbing form
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: attacked Cat-1 climb 65km out but faded final climb, suggests one-day power doesn't translate to stage race mountains
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: first visible weakness shown when Bahrain paced, 50-second gap to Landa opens after appearing strongest GC rider earlier
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: bridged to leaders and dropped van der Poel, but tactical error attacking 8km from finish on downhill
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: attacked summit but easily covered, moved to podium only because Almeida cracked not own acceleration
